skill.name: linear
skill.description: Guides AI agents in using the Linear CLI to manage issues, projects, teams, comments, links, and GraphQL workflows.
skill.summary: Use Linear from an AI agent through CLI recipes, issue workflows, project commands, and GraphQL examples.
skill.author: 0xBigBoss

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but review shows the package is Markdown documentation rather than executable code. Most command-execution alerts are false positives from examples, while real medium risks remain because the skill guides agents to run mutating Linear CLI commands, handle API keys, and upload files to Linear.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Executed Linear Mutations
medium_findings.description: The skill documents commands that create, update, delete, link, and comment on Linear issues and projects, often with the --yes confirmation flag. This is intended functionality, but an agent could modify production workspace data if the user does not explicitly approve the operation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines show real mutating Linear commands, including commands that bypass interactive confirmation. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but the impact is clear if commands run against the wrong workspace or issue.

medium_findings.title: Linear API Key and Local Config Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to configure LINEAR_API_KEY and references the local Linear config file. This is necessary for authentication, but API keys and config files are sensitive and can be exposed through shell history, logs, or broad file reads.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly references the API key environment variable and the config path. No exfiltration is shown, but these are confirmed sensitive credential-handling paths.

medium_findings.title: File Upload Through Signed Linear URLs
medium_findings.description: The GraphQL recipe requests a signed upload URL, uploads a local file with curl, and attaches the resulting asset URL to Linear. This can be safe when user-selected files are used, but it can leak local file contents if an agent uploads the wrong file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The upload workflow is documented end to end and includes a network PUT of local file data. The destination is Linear-controlled in context, so this is a moderate risk rather than evidence of malicious exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Static Analysis False Positives
low_findings.description: The many Ruby backtick and shell substitution alerts occur in Markdown inline examples or fenced command blocks. I found no Ruby source file or executable skill code that evaluates those snippets automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are clearly Markdown documentation and shell examples. They are not Ruby code and are not executed by the skill package itself.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Alerts Are Documentation Links or Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to Linear, Apollo Studio, GitHub pull request examples, and Linear status or documentation pages. I found no hidden third-party endpoint or data exfiltration destination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links or user-facing examples tied to Linear workflows. One example uses a GitHub PR URL as attachment content, but it is not an automated outbound request.

dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents commands that create, update, delete, link, and comment on Linear issues and projects, often with the --yes confirmation flag. This is intended functionality, but an agent could modify production workspace data if the user does not explicitly approve the operation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines show real mutating Linear commands, including commands that bypass interactive confirmation. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but the impact is clear if commands run against the wrong workspace or issue.

content.user_title: Manage Linear Issues from Your AI Agent
content.value_statement: Teams lose time switching between chat, code, and Linear. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code precise CLI workflows for Linear tasks.
actual_capabilities: List, search, view, create, update, link, comment on, and archive Linear issues through documented CLI commands.
List, view, create, update, archive, and connect Linear projects to issues.
Use Linear GraphQL recipes for attachments, comments, relations, parent issues, assignment, and file uploads.
Find team, issue, user, workflow state, and project identifiers needed for reliable Linear operations.
Troubleshoot authentication, empty results, mutation confirmation, GraphQL errors, connection issues, and config permissions.
limitations: Requires the separate @0xbigboss/linear-cli package to be installed and available on PATH.
Requires a valid Linear API key or configured Linear CLI authentication.
Mutating commands can change real Linear workspace data when the user approves them.
Some GraphQL recipes require UUIDs, schema knowledge, and careful variable selection.

use_cases.title: Developer Issue Updates
use_cases.description: Update issue state, assignment, priority, parent links, and comments while working in a coding agent session.

use_cases.title: Project Manager Triage
use_cases.description: Search issues, inspect teams and projects, create follow-up tasks, and link related work from one workflow.

use_cases.title: Automation QA for Linear CLI
use_cases.description: Run documented verification flows to check CLI behavior, mutation handling, GraphQL recipes, and troubleshooting coverage.

prompt_templates.title: List My Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Linear skill to list my assigned issues for TEAM_KEY. Show the issue identifiers, titles, states, and priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Linear issue in TEAM_KEY titled "TITLE" with this description: DESCRIPTION. Ask before running any mutating command.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Work Breakdown
prompt_templates.prompt: Review Linear issue ENG-123, propose child tasks, then create the approved tasks and link blockers only after confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Run GraphQL Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Linear GraphQL to fetch issue relations for ENG-123, identify missing parent or blocker links, and draft safe commands to fix them.

output_examples.input: Show my active Linear work for the engineering team.
output_examples.output: A concise list of assigned issues with identifiers, titles, workflow states, priorities, and direct Linear URLs.

output_examples.input: Create a parent issue and two child issues for the release checklist.
output_examples.output: A confirmation request, followed by created issue identifiers and a summary of parent-child relationships after approval.

output_examples.input: Find relations for ENG-123 and explain blockers.
output_examples.output: A readable relation summary showing parent issue, child issues, linked blockers, duplicate links, and suggested next actions.

best_practices: Ask for explicit user approval before running create, update, delete, link, comment, or upload commands.
Use redacted authentication checks and avoid pasting real API keys into shared prompts or logs.
Prefer built-in CLI commands before raw GraphQL, and use UUIDs where the documentation requires them.
anti_patterns: Do not run commands with --yes until the user approves the exact Linear change.
Do not upload local files unless the user names the file and confirms it should be attached.
Do not print unredacted config files, API keys, or authorization headers in agent output.

faq.question: Does this skill include its own Linear client?
faq.answer: No. It documents workflows for the separate @0xbigboss/linear-cli command-line tool.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it create or delete Linear data?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented CLI commands can create, update, link, comment on, and archive Linear resources.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The files are documentation, but they guide agents to run mutating commands, use credentials, and upload files.

faq.question: Does it require a Linear API key?
faq.answer: Yes. Users need Linear CLI authentication or a LINEAR_API_KEY value for API access.

faq.question: Should agents use GraphQL first?
faq.answer: No. The skill recommends built-in CLI commands first and raw GraphQL only when needed.
